en.extract:
Willcocks's honeyguide (Indicator willcocksi) is a species of bird in the family Indicatoridae.
It is found mainly throughout the African tropical rainforest.

The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the General Sir  James Willcocks.

'''Willcocks's honeyguide''' ('''''Indicator willcocksi''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Indicatoridae]].
It is found mainly throughout the [[Tropical forest|African tropical rainforest]].
[[File:Indicator willcocksi - Zac Peterson - 555846104.jpeg|left|thumb|On branch]]
The common name and Latin binomial commemorate the General Sir  [[James Willcocks]].<ref>{{cite book|last=Beolens|first=Bo|title=Whose Bird? Men and Women Commemorated in the Common Names of Birds|year=2003|publisher=Christopher Helm|location=London|page=367|author2=Watkins, Michael }}</ref>
